chunks.text: Joseph Gonzalez is an Associate Professor in the EECS department at UC Berkeley and a founding member of the UC Berkeley RISE Lab. His research interests are at the intersection of machine learning and data systems. His students work on projects including real-time model serving, machine learning life-cycle management, accelerated deep learning for computer vision, cryptographic primitives for federated learning, frameworks for deep reinforcement learning and parameter tuning, model based cloud resource management, software platforms for autonomous vehicles research, computational efficient representations for asynchronous time series, and smf frameworks for graph query processing.

Joseph Gonzalez is co-founder of Turi Inc. (formerly GraphLab), which was based on his thesis work on GraphLab and PowerGraph Systems. Turi was acquired by Apple Inc. Before joining UC Berkeley as an assistant professor, he was a post-doc in the UC Berkeley AMPLab working on projects including GraphX (now part of Apache Spark), MLbase, Velox, and concurrency control for ML. He obtained his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University's Machine Learning Department, working on Parallel and Distributed Systems for Probabilistic Reasoning.

Joseph Gonzalez's education includes a Ph.D. in Machine Learning from Carnegie Mellon University in 2012. His research areas are Artificial Intelligence (AI), Database Management Systems (DBMS), and Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT). He is affiliated with research centers including Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR), Berkeley Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence (RDI), Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD), EPIC Data lab, and Sky Computing Lab.

Joseph Gonzalez teaches CS 294-194: Research to Startup on Thursdays from 17:00-18:29 in Soda 310. Selected publications include 'FlorDB: Multiversion Hindsight Logging for Continuous Training' (2024), 'Representing Long-Range Context for Graph Neural Networks with Global Attention' (NeurIPS 2021), 'RLlib Flow: Distributed Reinforcement Learning is a Dataflow Problem' (NeurIPS 2021), 'Hindsight Logging for Model Training' (VLDB 2021), 'Checkmate: Breaking the Memory Wall with Optimal Tensor Rematerialization' (2020), and 'Optimistic concurrency control for distributed unsupervised learning' (NeurIPS 2013).

Selected honors and awards for Joseph Gonzalez include the Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science (2023), VLDB Test of Time Award (2023), Okawa Research Grant (2018), NASA Space Act Award (2005), and NASA Inventions and Contributions Board Award (2005). Contact information: 773 Soda Hall, jegonzal@eecs.berkeley.edu, with administrative contact Monica Naputi at mnaputi@berkeley.edu.
